summary: The patient has features consistent with dentinogenesis imperfecta including tooth browning, enamel wear without caries, pulp obliteration, narrow canals, and secondary dentin deposits—all supported by family history. This condition primarily involves defective dentin development due to genetic mutations affecting collagen type IV production, explaining both the structural abnormalities and inheritance pattern observed clinically.
